caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses moderate intermittent asthma.

evidence.span_text: 1. Moderate intermittent asthma.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician instructed the patient to use albuterol inhaler 1 to 2 puffs and monitor breathing prior to taking additional puffs.

evidence.span_text: - Medical Treatment: Prescribed provided for Flovent 110 mcg 1 puff twice per day. She can continue using her Ventolin inhaler 2 puffs every 4 to 6 hours as needed. I have recommended using her albuterol inhaler 1 to 2 puffs and then monitor her breathing prior to taking an additional 1 to 2 puffs. I am also going to put together an asthma action plan for her so we know what to do based on her symptoms.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician instructed the patient to use Ventolin inhaler 2 puffs every 4 to 6 hours as needed.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician instructed the patient to use Flovent 110 mcg 1 puff twice per day.

evidence.span_text: Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

caps.proposition_text: The patient reports asthma.

evidence.span_text: Asthma.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes no rubs, murmurs, or gallops.

evidence.span_text: - Auscultation of Heart: Regular rate and rhythm. No ectopic beats. No rubs, murmurs, or gallops.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes diminished lung sounds bilaterally.

evidence.span_text: - Auscultation of Lungs: Diminished lung sounds throughout with the occasional slight expiratory wheeze, bilaterally.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes regular rate and rhythm.

evidence.span_text: Cardiovascular

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes strong bilateral radial pulses.

evidence.span_text: - Examination: Strong bilateral radial pulses. No clubbing. Brisk capillary refill.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes neck is supple.

evidence.span_text: Neck

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes trachea is midline.

evidence.span_text: - General Examination: Neck is supple without lymphadenopathy. Trachea is midline. No carotid bruit.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes no carotid bruit.

caps.proposition_text: The patient will follow up in 1 month for reevaluation.

evidence.span_text: The patient will follow up in 1 month for reevaluation or sooner for worsening symptoms.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician prescribed Flovent 110 mcg.

I have recommended using her albuterol inhaler 1 to 2 puffs and then monitor her breathing prior to taking an additional 1 to 2 puffs. I am also going to put together an asthma action plan for her so we know what to do based on her symptoms.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician prescribed Ventolin inhaler.

I have recommended using her albuterol inhaler 1 to 2 puffs and then monitor her breathing prior to taking an additional 1 to 2 puffs. caps.proposition_text: The clinician prescribed albuterol inhaler.

evidence.span_text: - Patient Education and Counseling: I explained the importance of consistency with her daily inhaler as this will help prevent daily symptoms and the need to use the albuterol inhaler as frequently. My hope is that she has less acute exacerbations as she continues to use her inhaled steroid.
